task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS dashboard or main interface is visible, indicating the agent successfully logged in using the provided credentials.

task_completion.description: Periods 1, 2, and 3 are added to the schedule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The schedule list or table in OpenSIS displays rows with the titles 'Period 1', 'Period 2', and 'Period 3'.

task_completion.description: The Lunch period is added to the schedule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The schedule list or table in OpenSIS displays a row with the title 'Lunch'.

task_completion.description: Periods 4, 5, and 6 are added to the schedule.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The schedule list or table in OpenSIS displays rows with the titles 'Period 4', 'Period 5', and 'Period 6'.

task_completion.description: All created periods have the correct Sort Order, Short Name, and Length.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the final schedule list view, verify the columns for all 7 periods match the task table exactly: Sort Orders are 1-7, Short Names are P1-P3, LN, P4-P6, and Lengths are 55 for all numbered periods and 30 for Lunch.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the OpenSIS web interface to add periods rather than using direct database commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating OpenSIS menus, filling out web forms for school periods, and clicking save buttons, rather than using a terminal to execute SQL queries.

integrity.description: The final schedule list is a genuine result of saving the forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a logical progression of screenshots showing form filling and submission (e.g., 'Add Period' screens) leading to the final list, rather than browser developer tools being open to manipulate the DOM.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual OpenSIS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenSIS UI (menus, headers, specific styling for version 9.2) is consistently visible across screenshots, not a text editor or a mocked HTML table.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task is performed in OpenSIS Community Edition v9.2. The agent must configure exactly 7 periods in the school schedule interface. Verify the exact values in the OpenSIS schedule list: Period 1-3 (Length: 55, Short Name: P1-P3, Sort: 1-3), Lunch (Length: 30, Short Name: LN, Sort: 4), Period 4-6 (Length: 55, Short Name: P4-P6, Sort: 5-7).
